office_does: One of 49 state senators; writes and votes on state law, adopts state budgets, and confirms gubernatorial appointments for a four-year term.
race_blurb: Uncontested: incumbent Manka Dhingra is the only candidate who filed and advances to the November general election regardless of the primary result.

candidates.summary: Incumbent senator since 2017, Deputy Majority Leader, and chair of the Senate Law & Justice Committee, with a record centered on behavioral health and public safety policy.
highlights: 20 years as a King County senior deputy prosecutor, where she chaired the Therapeutic Alternative Unit [S1]
Deputy Majority Leader; chairs Senate Law & Justice; previously chaired the Behavioral Health Subcommittee [S1][S3][S4]
Has passed legislation on domestic violence, firearm violence prevention, senior property-tax relief, and criminal justice reform [S5]

experience.basis: Incumbent since 2017 with Senate leadership roles, committee chairmanship, and a substantial legislative record.

safety.basis: Prosecutorial background channeled into therapeutic alternatives, crisis-intervention training, behavioral-health expansion, and police accountability (SB 5066).

healthcare.basis: Focus on improving healthcare access and supporting community health centers within existing structures.

social.basis: Says she has led efforts to protect reproductive freedom and defend civil rights; endorsed by Planned Parenthood.

climate.basis: Pledges to 'fight for a safe and livable planet'; endorsed by Washington Conservation Action.

immigration.basis: Pledges to defend immigrants; endorsed by One America Votes.

spending.basis: Supports expanded behavioral-health and crisis-response systems and responsible investment in transportation.
